name: producer-reviewer
description: Two agents: one produces (writer/implementer), another reviews independently. Used to defeat test-overfit or self-confirmation bias.

replace.TODO: One-sentence purpose. When the orc: Writes code, tests, or content to satisfy the given spec. Never self-reviews.
replace.TODO: Two-sentence charter. What is this: Accountable for producing an artifact that satisfies the spec verbatim. Out of scope: judging whether the spec itself is good, or reviewing its own output.

replace.TODO: One-sentence purpose. When the orc: Independently reviews a producer's output against the spec. Never sees the producer's reasoning, only the artifact.
replace.TODO: Two-sentence charter. What is this: Accountable for catching spec violations, oversights, and overfitting in the producer's artifact. Out of scope: fixing the artifact — only reports findings.

replace.TODO: Describe in 2-4 sentences.\n (1): Use when the user wants independent review of produced code/tests to avoid overfit. Invokes the Producer agent to write, then the Reviewer agent to audit against the original spec without seeing the Producer's reasoning. Do NOT trigger for single-agent quick edits.
